params.name: customer
params.description: Customer

params.name: amount
params.description: Amount to be used for this test cash balance transaction. A positive integer representing how much to fund in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal) (e.g., 100 cents to fund $1.00 or 100 to fund ¥100, a zero-decimal currency).

params.name: currency
params.description: Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: reference
params.description: A description of the test funding. This simulates free-text references supplied by customers when making bank transfers to their cash balance. You can use this to test how Stripe's [reconciliation algorithm](https://docs.stripe.com/payments/customer-balance/reconciliation) applies to different user inputs.

tools.name: test-helpers_post-issuing-authorizations
tools.description:
Create a test-mode authorization.

. Required: card. Optional: amount, amount_details, authorization_method (plus 13 more). Returns the new IssuingAuthorization.

params.name: amount
params.description: The total amount to attempt to authorize. This amount is in the provided currency, or defaults to the card's currency, and in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).

params.name: amount_details
params.description: Detailed breakdown of amount components. These amounts are denominated in `currency` and in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).

params.name: authorization_method
params.description: How the card details were provided. Defaults to online.

params.name: card
params.description: Card associated with this authorization.

params.name: currency
params.description: The currency of the authorization. If not provided, defaults to the currency of the card. Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: fleet
params.description: Fleet-specific information for authorizations using Fleet cards.

params.name: fraud_disputability_likelihood
params.description: Probability that this transaction can be disputed in the event of fraud. Assessed by comparing the characteristics of the authorization to card network rules.

params.name: fuel
params.description: Information about fuel that was purchased with this transaction.

params.name: is_amount_controllable
params.description: If set `true`, you may provide [amount](https://docs.stripe.com/api/issuing/authorizations/approve#approve_issuing_authorization-amount) to control how much to hold for the authorization.

params.name: merchant_amount
params.description: The total amount to attempt to authorize. This amount is in the provided merchant currency, and in the [smallest currency unit](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies#zero-decimal).

params.name: merchant_currency
params.description: The currency of the authorization. If not provided, defaults to the currency of the card. Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).

params.name: merchant_data
params.description: Details about the seller (grocery store, e-commerce website, etc.

params.name: network_data
params.description: Details about the authorization, such as identifiers, set by the card network.

params.name: risk_assessment
params.description: Stripe’s assessment of the fraud risk for this authorization.

params.name: verification_data
params.description: Verifications that Stripe performed on information that the cardholder provided to the merchant.

params.name: wallet
params.description: The digital wallet used for this transaction. One of `apple_pay`, `google_pay`, or `samsung_pay`. Will populate as `null` when no digital wallet was utilized.

tools.name: tokens_post
tools.description:
Creates a single-use token that represents a bank account’s details. You can use this token with any v1 API method in place of a bank account dictionary. You can only use this token once. To do so, attach it to a connected account where controller.requirement_collection is application, which includes Custom accounts.

. Optional: account, bank_account, card (plus 5 more). Returns the new Token.

params.name: account
params.description: Information for the account this token represents.

params.name: bank_account
params.description: The bank account this token will represent.

params.name: card
params.description: The card this token will represent. If you also pass in a customer, the card must be the ID of a card belonging to the customer. Otherwise, if you do not pass in a customer, this is a dictionary containing a user's credit card details, with the options described below.

params.name: customer
params.description: Create a token for the customer, which is owned by the application's account. You can only use this with an [OAuth access token](https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts) or [Stripe-Account header](https://docs.stripe.com/connect/authentication). Learn more about [cloning saved payment methods](https://docs.stripe.com/connect/cloning-saved-payment-methods).

params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: pii

tools.name: transfers_post
tools.description:
To send funds from your Stripe account to a connected account, you create a new transfer object. Your Stripe balance must be able to cover the transfer amount, or you’ll receive an “Insufficient Funds” error.

. Required: currency, destination. Optional: amount, description, expand (plus 4 more). Returns the new Transfer.

params.name: amount
params.description: A positive integer in cents (or local equivalent) representing how much to transfer.

params.name: currency
params.description: Three-letter [ISO code for currency](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html) in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://docs.stripe.com/currencies).

params.name: description
params.description: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

params.name: destination
params.description: The ID of a connected Stripe account. See the Connect documentation for details.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.

params.name: source_transaction
params.description: You can use this parameter to transfer funds from a charge before they are added to your available balance. A pending balance will transfer immediately but the funds will not become available until the original charge becomes available. [See the Connect documentation](https://docs.stripe.com/connect/separate-charges-and-transfers#transfer-availability) for details.

params.name: source_type
params.description: The source balance to use for this transfer. One of `bank_account`, `card`, or `fpx`. For most users, this will default to `card`.

params.name: transfer_group
params.description: A string that identifies this transaction as part of a group. See the [Connect documentation](https://docs.stripe.com/connect/separate-charges-and-transfers#transfer-options) for details.

tools.name: transfers_reversals_post-transfers-id
tools.description:
When you create a new reversal, you must specify a transfer to create it on.

When reversing transfers, you can optionally reverse part of the transfer. You can do so as many times as you wish until the entire transfer has been reversed.

Once entirely reversed, a transfer can’t be reversed again. This method will return an error when called on an already-reversed transfer, or when trying to reverse more money than is left on a transfer.

. Required: id. Optional: amount, description, expand (plus 2 more). Returns the new TransferReversal.

params.name: id
params.description: Id

params.name: amount
params.description: A positive integer in cents (or local equivalent) representing how much of this transfer to reverse. Can only reverse up to the unreversed amount remaining of the transfer. Partial transfer reversals are only allowed for transfers to Stripe Accounts. Defaults to the entire transfer amount.

params.name: description
params.description: An arbitrary string which you can attach to a reversal object. This will be unset if you POST an empty value.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.

params.name: refund_application_fee
params.description: Boolean indicating whether the application fee should be refunded when reversing this transfer. If a full transfer reversal is given, the full application fee will be refunded. Otherwise, the application fee will be refunded with an amount proportional to the amount of the transfer reversed.

Maximum 10 characters for `ach` payments, 140 characters for `us_domestic_wire` payments, or 500 characters for `stripe` network transfers. Can only include -#.$&*, spaces, and alphanumeric characters. The default value is 'payment'.

tools.name: webhook-endpoints_post
tools.description:
A webhook endpoint must have a url and a list of enabled_events. You may optionally specify the Boolean connect parameter. If set to true, then a Connect webhook endpoint that notifies the specified url about events from all connected accounts is created; otherwise an account webhook endpoint that notifies the specified url only about events from your account is created. You can also create webhook endpoints in the webhooks settings section of the Dashboard.

. Required: enabled_events, url. Optional: api_version, connect, description (plus 2 more). Returns the new WebhookEndpoint.

params.name: api_version
params.description: Events sent to this endpoint will be generated with this Stripe Version instead of your account's default Stripe Version.

params.name: connect
params.description: Whether this endpoint should receive events from connected accounts (`true`), or from your account (`false`). Defaults to `false`.

params.name: description
params.description: An optional description of what the webhook is used for.

params.name: enabled_events
params.description: The list of events to enable for this endpoint. You may specify `['*']` to enable all events, except those that require explicit selection.

params.name: expand
params.description: Specifies which fields in the response should be expanded.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.

params.name: url
params.description: The URL of the webhook endpoint.

params.name: expand

params.description: Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.
